transcript: [accented] I'm Dawson Yassen, and I'm... [dropping accent] ...trying to gather some information about the election.
You know, you called here twice in the last minute, fella, and you'd better stop.
And who I vote for, of... or my wife, is none of your damn business.
I got call waitin'. I asked you to call, I'm gonna report you. Now stop calling.
Do you want to go vote together?
Yes, I was just calling to ask who you voted for?
Um... I don't really think that's any of your business.
Well, I'll give you four hundred and fifty dollars. [exhales]
I'm sure you will. And, let me tell you one other thing. You call back here, and you'll be paying somebody else four hundred and fifty dollars. Like a lawyer.
Yeah. Uh huh. Have a nice evening.
I need you to tell me who you voted for, or if you... or if you even voted.
"Who'd I vote for, pal"? I voted for Mao Zedong.
And Bin Laden. Binny. Bin... actually, Binny's a friend of mine, and, uh... we've known each other since we were in politics together.
I'm an in... a registered independent.
I am a Marxist-Socialist-Leninist-Communist-Christian... let's see, what else... uh, American Socialist Worker's Party-Union Member-Republican.
I'm 'onna whoop ya in the ass... come Election Day. How's that sound?

You need to get back into that stew you've been in. I don't usually give you the bus... people this much time, but... uh, this is rather enjoyable. Now, where... who... wh... where'd you get this number?
I'm 'onna whoop ya in the ass.
[laughing] Go... go back to your stew! [laughs]
Yes, who... I was wondering who you voted for?
Uh, I think I asked you not to call here. I think I asked you not to call here twice. And I'm not... and I won't ask again.
I was calling to find out who you were going to vote for?
That is none of your business. Don't call again, you called us eight times, we're sick of it.
No, I didn't in... even call you before.
Oh, yes you have. Don't call again.
Yes, I was just calling to ask who you voted for?
Well... that's not the nature of the call.
[laughing] Well, what the hell is the nature of the call?
I'll give you four hundred and fifty dollars for the information.
Bullshit. You tell me who I should vote for, and I'm goin' with the opposite. Who should I vote for?
Have I got your solemn word, ma'am?
Well, I need to know who you plan on votin' for.
What do you want to know?
Uh, you don't give me nothin'.
Just calling to gather who you voted for.
Uh, I don't think that's any of your business. Who... who are you affiliated with?
'[sampled]' I ain't workin' at no lumber yard!
I don't give a shit who you are, it's none of your business who I voted for!
Hi, I'm Ollie. And I was calling to ask who you voted for.
Can I ask why, please, ma'am?
Because I don't give a fuck.
I'm calling to ask who you're voting for...
You're just a fucking... annoying dude.
Hello? This is Dick calling, and I was just calling to ask who you were voting for.
Oh, that's private. And, you just called this number, I think. Do you have a phone number?
Could you tell it to me?
Well, I'm just... I'm the one calling you--

No, I'm asking you. If you want to ask me a personal question, I think I should be able to ask you one, and I think that thing you just said was obnoxious. But since I got caller ID - alright? I might not need you to answer the question for me. I think that was kind of an obnoxious response.
And I don't think you need to be calling this number, and if you call this number again...
...I'm gonna press charges against you, do you understand?
Well, I'll give you four hundred and fifty dollars for the information.
You'll pay me... [scoffs] ...to tell you who I will vote for? That's illegal.
Why are you calling here? Are you... do you... do you need a doctor?
I just want to know who you're vo...
No, I'm asking you, are you seeing a psychiatrist? Because you really should be calling them and not me.
What is your... deal here, tonight?
Sir, you've called me three times and I've asked you not to call, what is your deal?
Do you not respect an individual? Do you know my name?
You need to talk to your doctor! Do you understand me?
You're not supposed to be calling three times in a row. If they don't want to talk to you, they don't want to talk to you. Why do you keep calling them?
I want to know who you're voting for.
That's harassment. [laughs incredulously] That's none of your business!
You need to talk to your doctor.
I think that tone is harassment.
Sir, if you call here again, I'm gonna press charges against you. There is something very seriously wrong with you, and you need to see your doctor.
And I got too much at stake ridin' on this, here.
You do? Are you an alcoholic too, sir?

A person's vote is their private business. You have called this number, this is your third time. You've been asked not to call, and I don't understand what your mind is thinking, and I have no idea and I really don't care.
I'm going to pay you to tell me who you're voting for.
You're not going to pay me, no.
I'll pay you a visit and whoop ya in the ass.
Okay, I'm getting on the other phone and call... [in background] Mom, I need the cell phone, 'cause I wanna call the police.
Uh, what you just heard, sir, I'm calling the police, and I'm having them trace this call.
I'm on my cell phone and having the police trace this call.
Good. You're committing the crime, buddy.
